headline: Nvidia Earnings Likely to Shine Even as Stock Underperforms
summary: Artificial-intelligence leader Nvidia has a big task when it reports first-quarter earnings later today: convince investors that the AI investment boom still has legs and that Nvidia won’t lose its pole position.  Wall Street analysts expect the company to report earnings per share of $1.75, up from 81 cents a year ago.  Nvidia remains the biggest winner of the AI age.

headline: Exclusive-Meta CEO tells employees he does not expect more company-wide layoffs this year
summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg told employees in an internal ‌memo on Wednesday that he does not expect more ‌company-wide layoffs this year, according to a copy of the memo ​seen by Reuters.  He made the announcement on the same day the Facebook owner carried out a massive restructuring of the company, laying off 10% of its workforce globally and ‌transferring 7,000 other ⁠employees to new initiatives related to AI workflows.  The changes are part of a far-reaching overhaul taking place at Meta this year, as the company surges its AI investments in a bid to center AI ‌agents in both its product offerings ​and its approach to work ​internally.

content.title: Exclusive-Meta CEO tells employees he does not expect more company-wide layoffs this year
content.summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg told employees in an internal ‌memo on Wednesday that he does not expect more ‌company-wide layoffs this year, according to a copy of the memo ​seen by Reuters.  He made the announcement on the same day the Facebook owner carried out a massive restructuring of the company, laying off 10% of its workforce globally and ‌transferring 7,000 other ⁠employees to new initiatives related to AI workflows.  The changes are part of a far-reaching overhaul taking place at Meta this year, as the company surges its AI investments in a bid to center AI ‌agents in both its product offerings ​and its approach to work ​internally.

headline: Hedge Funds Followed the April Equity Rally by Increasing Exposure to Technology Leaders, Hazeltree Finds

Hedge funds continued to concentrate holdings in companies characterized by superior fundamentals, strong profitability, high return on equity and leading margins, according to Hazeltree, a leading provider of integrated treasury and liquidity management solutions for alternative asset managers.

This multiple sits below both the three-year average of 25 and the three-year peak of 33. headline: India's Nykaa seeks to include Meta in music copyright fight with Zee
summary: Indian fashion-to-beauty retailer Nykaa has asked a New Delhi court ‌to make Meta a party to a copyright ‌dispute filed by media firm Zee, court documents show, in a case ​seen as having implications for the commercial use of music on Instagram.  • Zee Entertainment has sued Nykaa for allegedly using its copyrighted songs in Instagram reels to promote its products, ‌seeking $210,000 in damages, ⁠Reuters reported this month.  • In a non-public filing dated May 19 and seen by Reuters ⁠on Wednesday, Nykaa told the Delhi High Court that Meta must be included, as only it can "authoritatively state whether use" ​of music ​clips by Nykaa breached any ​licence terms.

content.title: India's Nykaa seeks to include Meta in music copyright fight with Zee
content.summary: Indian fashion-to-beauty retailer Nykaa has asked a New Delhi court ‌to make Meta a party to a copyright ‌dispute filed by media firm Zee, court documents show, in a case ​seen as having implications for the commercial use of music on Instagram.  • Zee Entertainment has sued Nykaa for allegedly using its copyrighted songs in Instagram reels to promote its products, ‌seeking $210,000 in damages, ⁠Reuters reported this month.  • In a non-public filing dated May 19 and seen by Reuters ⁠on Wednesday, Nykaa told the Delhi High Court that Meta must be included, as only it can "authoritatively state whether use" ​of music ​clips by Nykaa breached any ​licence terms.

headline: Nvidia Has to Prove the AI Boom Still Has Legs—and Its Still at the Top
summary: Artificial-intelligence leader Nvidia has a big task when it reports first-quarter earnings later today: convince investors that the AI investment boom still has legs and that Nvidia won’t lose its pole position.  Wall Street analysts expect the company to report earnings per share of $1.75, up from 81 cents a year ago.  Nvidia remains the biggest winner of the AI age.
